title: Alwar Fort
tagline: Alwar Fort — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: Bala Quila also known Alwar Fort is a fort in Alwar in Indian state of Rajasthan.
overview.description: Bala Quila also known Alwar Fort is a fort in Alwar in Indian state of Rajasthan. It is situated on a hill in the Aravalli Range, above the town of Alwar. The fort is 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) long and is about 1.5 kilometres (0.93 mi) wide. Bala fort was originally constructed by Nikumbh Rajputs who were one of ancient rulers in Rajasthan and belongs to suryavanshi clan.

topPlaces.name: Government Museum, Alwar
topPlaces.description: Government Museum, Alwar is a museum located in the Vinay Vilas Mahal of City Palace Alwar in Rajasthan, India. The museum collection captures the cultural, historical, and military past of Rajputana.

topPlaces.name: Indira Gandhi Stadium (Alwar)
topPlaces.description: Indira Gandhi Stadium is a multi-purpose stadium used mostly for association football and field hockey and also for athletics in Alwar, Rajasthan, India. The ground first held a first-class match in December 1993 when Rajasthan played the Vidarbha in the 1993/94 Ranji Trophy.

topPlaces.name: Jagannath Temple, Alwar
topPlaces.description: Jagannath Temple (Devnagari: जगन्नाथ मन्दिर) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu and located in Alwar, India. The temple's presiding deity is anthropomorphic form of Lord Jagannath of Puri, while other two deities are SitaramJi, the Shaligram maharaj and JankiJi, the goddess Laxmi.

topPlaces.name: Alwar
topPlaces.description: Alwar (Rajasthani Pronunciation: [əlʋəɾ]) is a city in Rajasthan and the administrative headquarters of Alwar District in the state of Rajasthan. It is located 150 km south of Delhi and 150 km north of Jaipur.

topPlaces.name: Siliserh Lake
topPlaces.description: Siliserh Lake is an artificial lake situated eight miles southwest of Alwar. It covers an area of about 7km2 and is flanked by dense woodland and cenotaphs on its embankment.

items.note: Government Museum, Alwar is a museum located in the Vinay Vilas Mahal of City Palace Alwar in Rajasthan, India. The museum collection captures the cultural, historical, and military past of Rajputana.

Indira Gandhi Stadium is a multi-purpose stadium used mostly for association football and field hockey and also for athletics in Alwar, Rajasthan, India. The ground first held a first-class match in December 1993 when Rajasthan played the Vidarbha in the 1993/94 Ranji Trophy.

Jagannath Temple (Devnagari: जगन्नाथ मन्दिर) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu and located in Alwar, India. The temple's presiding deity is anthropomorphic form of Lord Jagannath of Puri, while other two deities are SitaramJi, the Shaligram maharaj and JankiJi, the goddess Laxmi.

Alwar (Rajasthani Pronunciation: [əlʋəɾ]) is a city in Rajasthan and the administrative headquarters of Alwar District in the state of Rajasthan. It is located 150 km south of Delhi and 150 km north of Jaipur.
Siliserh Lake is an artificial lake situated eight miles southwest of Alwar. It covers an area of about 7km2 and is flanked by dense woodland and cenotaphs on its embankment.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Alwar Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Alwar Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Alwar Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jaipur International Airport (113 km) and the nearest railway station is Jaipur Junction (107 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Alwar Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Alwar Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Alwar Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Alwar Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Alwar Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Government Museum, Alwar, Indira Gandhi Stadium (Alwar), Jagannath Temple, Alwar, Alwar, Siliserh Lake.
